rulings.Case Title: C.I. ENERGIA SOLAR S.A., INC., VS. BAGATELOS GLASS SYSTEMS, INC., ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Plaintiff C.I. Energia Solar S.A. And E.S. Windows' Person Most Knowledgable And Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursay August 1, 2019 line 3 - DEFENDANT BAGATELOS GLASS SYSTEMS, INC.,'s Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Plaintiff C.I. Energia Solar S.A. And E.S. Windows' Person Most Knowledgable And Production Of Documents. Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidmcdonald@prodigy.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: TORRIE SPIKENER ET AL VS. DIGNITY HEALTH (DBA ST. FRANCIS MEMORIAL HOSPITAL)ETAL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on the Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, August 1, 2019, line 5. DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Defendant The Regents of the University of California's motion for summary judgment is denied. "'When a defendant moves for summary judgment and supports his motion with expert declarations that his conduct fell within the community standard of care, he is entitled to summary judgment unless the plaintiff comes forward with conflicting expert evidence.'" (Munro v. Regents of University of California (1989) 215 Cal.App.3d 977, 985.) In this case, the declaration of Dr. McGuire shows that The Regents' doctors were acting in a consultative fashion and complied with the standard of care. (McGuire Decl. 8, 9.) In opposition, the declaration of Dr. Frankel creates a triable issue of material fact regarding whether they breached the standard of care by not sufficiently following up on decedent Geovonni Mabrey's care and by advising against the use of antibiotics. (Frankel Decl. 7-12.) "[W]hen considering the declarations of the parties' experts, we liberally construe the declarations for the [opposing party's] experts and resolve any doubts as to the propriety of granting the motion in favor of the [opposing party]." (Powell v. Kleinman (2007) 151 Cal.App.4th 112, 125-126 [reversing summary judgment for defendants in medical malpractice case where opposing party's expert declaration established triable issues of material fact on breach of standard of care and causation].) There are also triable issues of fact as to causation. (Compare McGuire Decl. 9 with Frankel Decl. 11.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Dr. Ellis is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: VICTORIA BOYKO VS. DAVID J MCMAHON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-19-575298, CGC-18-571704 WITH CASE CGC-18-569759
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on the Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, August 1, 2019, line 7. DEFENDANTS DAVID MCMAHON, LAW OFFICES OF DAVID J. MCMAHON'S MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-19-575298, CGC-18-571704 WITH CASE CGC-18-569759. Defendants and cross-complainants David McMahon and Law Offices of David J. McMahon's motion to consolidate for trial only cases Nos. CGC-18-569759, CGC-18-571704, and CGC-19-575298 is granted. The three actions involve common questions of law and fact: most of the legal claims stated by the three plaintiffs in their respective complaints are identical, and arise out of defendants' alleged oral promises to plaintiffs and defendants' alleged misclassification of the plaintiffs who performed similar work for defendants during the same period of time. (Code Civ. Proc., § 1048.) Consolidation for trial purposes will serve judicial economy and efficiency and avoid duplicative or inconsistent rulings. (See Sanchez v. Superior Court (1988) 203 Cal.App.3d 1391, 1396 ["In a consolidation for trial, the pleadings, verdicts, findings and judgments are kept separate; the actions are simply tried together for the sake of convenience and judicial economy"].) Plaintiff has not shown that a continuance of the date of her trial would be so prejudicial that it would outweigh the factors favoring consolidation. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendants is required to prepare a proposed order repeating the tentative verbatim and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: LINDA COOKS VS. SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on the Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, August 1, 2019, line 8. DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. *** Tentative Ruling - Part 1 of 2 *** Defendant San Francisco Unified School District's demurrers to the first, second and third causes of action for race, age, and sex discrimination and the sixth cause of action for breach of the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing are sustained without leave to amend. To plead a prima facie case of discrimination, the plaintiff must allege that "(1) he was a member of a protected class, (2) he was qualified for the position he sought or was performing competently in the position he held, (3) he suffered an adverse employment action, such as termination, demotion, or denial of an available job, and (4) some other circumstance suggests discriminatory motive." (Guz v. Bechtel Nat'l, Inc. (2000) 24 Cal.4th 317, 355.) Plaintiff alleges that she was qualified for several non-temporary teaching positions based on her work experience and education. (Amended Complaint, 17-18.) However, in the July 27, 2017 release agreement that plaintiff attaches to the amended complaint, she expressly agreed that "she will not apply for nor accept any position at the district now or in the future, other than day-to-day substitute teacher." (Id., Ex. B.) Plaintiff does not plead any facts showing that the release is unenforceable, and in fact relies upon the agreement to support her sixth cause of action. (See Skrbina v. Fleming Companies (1996) 45 Cal.App.4th 1353, 1367 ["In general, a written release extinguishes any obligation covered by the release's terms, provided it has not been obtained by fraud, deception, misrepresentation, duress, or undue influence. [Citations.] 'The general rule is that when a person with the capacity of reading and understanding an instrument signs it, he is, in the absence of fraud and imposition, bound by its contents, and is estopped from saying that its provisions are contrary to his intentions or understanding'"] [plaintiff's employment discrimination action was barred by written release].) Nor does plaintiff allege that she was treated any differently than any similarly-situated person (that is, another person who expressly agreed not to apply for or accept a full-time position). Under the circumstances, whether because she was not "qualified" or "eligible" for full-time employment or because she is estopped, she cannot claim that the District's decisions not to hire her for full-time positions that she agreed not to apply for or accept was discriminatory. *** End of Part 1 - Tentative Ruling continues in Part 2 of 2 *** =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: LINDA COOKS VS. SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant San Francisco Unified School District'S Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint (Added For Purposes Of Entering Tentative Ruling
rulings.Rulings: Defendant San Francisco Unified School District's Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint *** Tentative Ruling - Part 2 of 2 *** The demurrer to the sixth cause of action for breach of the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff alleges that the parties agreed that she would work as a substitute teacher but defendant "mistreated" her as a full time teacher by hiring her to teach a class for more than 30 days, requiring her to grade the students' work and create assignments, yet paying her like a substitute teacher. (Amended Complaint, 67-68.) That claim is inconsistent with the governing statutory scheme. The Education Code prescribes four classifications for certificated teachers, such as plaintiff: (1) permanent, (2) probationary, (3) substitute and (4) temporary. (Educ. Code §§ 44915, 44919.) "A former permanent teacher rehired as a substitute teacher is not entitled to permanent status while employed as a substitute." Edwards v. Lake Elsinore Unified School Dist. (2014) 230 Cal.App.4th 1532, 1543.) Contrary to plaintiff's allegations, she was not entitled to be paid as a permanent teacher or to be free of the usual duties of a teacher such as grading and creating assignments merely because she was hired as a substitute for more than 30 days. (See id. at 1540-1546 [holding that teacher who was hired by school district as a substitute for an entire school year was properly classified and paid as a substitute and therefore was not entitled to backpay].) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendant is required to prepare a proposed order repeating the tentative verbatim and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. *** End of Tentative Ruling *** =(302/EPS)
